title: Dividend Aristocrats Analysis 2026: Yield, Payout & Growth
description: 68 companies have raised dividends for 25+ consecutive years. But not all Aristocrats are created equal — some yield 5x more than others.

schemas.headline: Dividend Aristocrats Analysis 2026: Yield, Payout & Growth
schemas.description: Complete analysis of S&P 500 Dividend Aristocrats — companies with 25+ consecutive years of dividend increases. Current yields, P/E ratios, market caps, and sector breakdown.

acceptedAnswer.text: A Dividend Aristocrat is a company in the S&P 500 index that has increased its dividend payout every year for at least 25 consecutive years. As of 2026, there are 69 Dividend Aristocrats. These companies have demonstrated consistent profitability and commitment to returning capital to shareholders through decades of market cycles, recessions, and economic uncertainty.

mainEntity.name: Which Dividend Aristocrat has the highest yield?

acceptedAnswer.text: The highest yielding Dividend Aristocrat can vary based on market conditions, but typical leaders have yields exceeding 5%. High yield alone isn't always the best indicator — investors should also consider payout ratio sustainability, earnings growth, and the company's competitive position.

Dividend Aristocrats have historically performed well with lower volatility. Their 25+ year track record of dividend increases demonstrates financial discipline and durable competitive advantages. They provide a growing income stream that can help offset inflation. However, past performance doesn't guarantee future results, and some aristocrats trade at premium valuations.

mainEntity.name: What is the average yield of Dividend Aristocrats?

acceptedAnswer.text: The average dividend yield across all 69 Dividend Aristocrats is 2.54% as of 2026.
